39.7: Then he blinded Zedekiah. The last thing Zedekiah saw was the death of his sons. They put him in bronze chains and took him to Babylon.
39.8: The Chaldeans burned the royal palace and the houses of the people and demolished the walls of Jerusalem.
39.9: Then Nebuzaradan, the captain of the guard, deported to Babylon everyone left in the city — the survivors, the deserters who had already come over, and the remaining population.
39.10: Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left behind the poorest of the poor — those who had nothing — and gave them vineyards and fields right then and there.
39.11: Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon had issued specific orders about Jeremiah through Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard:
39.12: Find him, protect him, and do him no harm. Give him whatever he asks.
39.13: So Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard, Nebushazban the Rab-saris, Nergalsharezer the Rab-mag, and all of Babylon's senior commanders
39.14: had Jeremiah brought out of the court of the guard and released to Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan, who took him home. And Jeremiah settled among his own people.
39.15: While Jeremiah was still confined in the court of the guard, Yahweh's word had come to him:
39.16: Go and tell Ebed-melech the Ethiopian: This is what Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, says — I am bringing everything I said against this city — disaster, not salvation — and it will be accomplished before your eyes on that day.
39.17: But on that day I will rescue you — Yahweh declares it — and you will not be handed over to the men you are afraid of.
39.18: I will absolutely save you. You will not die by the sword. Your life will be your prize — because you put your whole trust in me. Yahweh's word.

43.1: When Jeremiah had finished delivering to the people every word Yahweh their God had sent him to speak —
43.2: Azariah son of Hoshaiah and Johanan son of Kareah and all the arrogant men said to Jeremiah: You are lying! Yahweh our God did not send you to say, 'Do not go to Egypt to live there.'
43.3: It is Baruch son of Neriah who is setting you against us — trying to hand us over to the Chaldeans, who will kill us or drag us off to Babylon.
43.4: So Johanan son of Kareah and all the military commanders and all the people refused to obey Yahweh's voice and stay in the land of Judah.
43.5: Instead, Johanan son of Kareah and all the military commanders gathered every last survivor of Judah who had come back from all the nations where they had been scattered — those who had returned to live in Judah —
43.6: men, women, and children, the king's daughters, everyone Nebuzaradan the commander of the guard had assigned to Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan — Jeremiah the prophet and Baruch son of Neriah among them —
43.7: They went to Egypt — straight into disobedience to Yahweh's word — and came all the way to Tahpanhes.
43.8: In Tahpanhes, Yahweh's word came to Jeremiah:
43.9: Take some large stones and bury them in the mortar of the brick terrace at the entrance to Pharaoh's palace here in Tahpanhes — do it where the men of Judah can see you.
43.10: Then tell them: Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, says this: I am summoning Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon — my instrument — and placing his throne directly over these stones I have buried here. He will spread his royal pavilion above them.
43.11: When he comes, he will strike Egypt down. For those appointed to death — death. For those appointed to captivity — captivity. For those appointed to the sword — the sword.
43.12: I will set fire to the temples of Egypt's gods. He will burn them and carry the images off as plunder. He will strip Egypt clean the way a shepherd strips his garment clean, and he will leave without opposition.
43.13: He will smash the sacred obelisks of Heliopolis and burn to the ground every temple of Egypt's gods.

44.1: This is the word Yahweh gave to Jeremiah regarding all the Jews settled throughout Egypt — at Migdol, at Tahpanhes, at Memphis, and throughout the region of Pathros:
44.2: Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, says this: You have seen every disaster I brought down on Jerusalem and every town of Judah. Today they lie in ruins, without a single inhabitant —
44.3: all because of the evil they chose: they provoked my fury by going after foreign gods and burning offerings to them — gods their ancestors never knew, gods you never knew.
44.4: And yet I sent you my servants the prophets — sent them early and sent them often — crying: Do not do this abomination! I hate it!
44.5: But they would not listen. They would not even turn their heads. They kept on in their wickedness, kept on burning offerings to foreign gods.
44.6: So my fury and my anger poured out and burned through every city of Judah and every street of Jerusalem — and they became the desolation that stands before you today.
44.7: So now — Yahweh, the God of hosts, the God of Israel, says this: Why are you doing this terrible thing to yourselves? Why are you cutting off from Judah every man and woman, every infant and child, leaving no survivor?
44.8: You are provoking me with your own hands by burning offerings to foreign gods here in Egypt — guaranteeing that you will be wiped out, a byword and a disgrace among every nation on earth.
44.9: Have you already forgotten the evil your ancestors committed, the evil of Judah's kings and their wives, and your own evil and the evil of your wives — everything that happened in Judah's cities and Jerusalem's streets?
44.10: Even now, not one of them has been humbled. Not one has feared me. Not one has walked in my law and my statutes that I laid out clearly before you and your ancestors.
44.11: Therefore Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, says this: I am turning my face against you — for ruin, not rescue — to cut Judah off completely.
44.12: I will take the whole remnant of Judah — those who set their faces toward Egypt and came here to settle — and they will be destroyed. In Egypt they will fall. By sword and famine they will be devoured, from the smallest to the greatest. They will become a source of cursing and horror, a byword of contempt.
44.13: I will visit Egypt's settlers with the same judgment I brought on Jerusalem — sword, famine, and plague.
44.14: No one from Judah's remnant who came to Egypt to settle will escape or survive to go home to Judah — though they long for it. No one will return. Only a few refugees will slip through.
44.15: Then all the men who knew their wives had been burning offerings to foreign gods, and all the women who stood there — a vast crowd — and all the people of Pathros in Egypt, answered Jeremiah:
44.16: The word you have spoken to us in Yahweh's name — we will not obey it.
44.17: We will do everything we vowed: burn offerings to the queen of heaven and pour out drink offerings to her, exactly as we and our fathers and our kings and our officials did in every city of Judah and in every street of Jerusalem. In those days we had food in abundance, we prospered, and no disaster touched us.
44.18: But the moment we stopped burning offerings to the queen of heaven and pouring out drink offerings to her, we lost everything. The sword and famine have been devouring us ever since.
44.19: The women added: When we burned offerings to the queen of heaven and poured out drink offerings to her, did we shape the cakes in her image and pour out those offerings without our husbands knowing? Did we act on our own?
44.20: Jeremiah replied to all the people — men and women alike, everyone who had answered him:
44.21: The offerings you burned in Judah's cities and Jerusalem's streets — you and your ancestors, your kings and officials and the common people — do you think Yahweh forgot them? Do you think he did not notice?
44.22: Yahweh could endure your evil deeds and your abominations no longer — and that is exactly why your land has become the desolation and the horror and the curse it is today, empty of all inhabitants.
44.23: Because you burned those offerings and sinned against Yahweh — because you refused to obey his voice, to walk in his law, his statutes, and his testimonies — this disaster has fallen on you. Everything you see today is the result.
44.24: Jeremiah continued — addressing all the people, and the women in particular: Hear Yahweh's word, all of you from Judah who are living here in Egypt.
44.25: Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, says this: You and your wives have declared openly — declared it aloud and backed it with your hands — 'We will make good our vows to the queen of heaven; we will burn offerings and pour out drink offerings.' Very well. Confirm your vows. Carry them out.
44.26: Therefore hear Yahweh's word, all of Judah in Egypt: I have sworn by my own great name — Yahweh declares it — that no one from Judah anywhere in Egypt will ever again swear by my name, saying 'As the Lord GOD lives.'
44.27: I am watching over them for ruin, not rescue. Every man of Judah here in Egypt will be consumed by sword and famine until the last of them is gone.
44.28: A handful who survive the sword will make their way back from Egypt to Judah. And the entire remnant of Judah who came to Egypt to settle will find out whose word stands — mine or theirs.
44.29: And here is the sign I will give you — Yahweh declares it — proof that I will carry out my judgment against you right here in Egypt. You will know that every word I have spoken against you will stand:
44.30: Yahweh says this: I am handing Pharaoh Hophra king of Egypt over to his enemies — to every faction that wants him dead — just as I handed Zedekiah king of Judah to Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, who was his enemy and came for his life.

45.1: This is the word Jeremiah the prophet spoke to Baruch son of Neriah when Baruch was writing down these words from Jeremiah's dictation — in the fourth year of Jehoiakim son of Josiah, king of Judah:
45.2: Yahweh, the God of Israel, says this to you, Baruch:
45.3: You have said, 'I am undone! Yahweh has piled grief on top of grief. I am worn out with groaning and can find no rest.'
45.4: Say this to him: Yahweh says — what I built, I am now demolishing; what I planted, I am now tearing up. The whole land. All of it.
45.5: As for you — are you looking for something great for yourself? Stop looking. I am bringing disaster on every living thing. Yahweh declares it. But this much I give you: you will escape with your life wherever you go.

46.1: This is the word Yahweh gave to Jeremiah the prophet concerning the nations:
46.2: Concerning Egypt: concerning the army of Pharaoh Neco, king of Egypt, stationed at the Euphrates at Carchemish — the army Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon crushed in the fourth year of Jehoiakim son of Josiah, king of Judah:
46.3: Get your shields ready — small and large — and advance to the line!
46.4: Saddle the horses! Cavalry, mount up! Helmets on — take your positions! Sharpen the spears! Put on your armor!
46.5: But what is this I see? They are in panic, turning and running. Their best warriors are cut down and fleeing headlong — not one of them looks back. Terror on every side! Yahweh declares it.
46.6: The swift cannot outrun this. The strongest cannot escape. They stumble and fall at the Euphrates, there in the north.
46.7: Who is this rising like the flooding Nile, swelling like rivers in flood?
46.8: Egypt — surging like the Nile, swelling like flood-swollen rivers. Egypt said: I will rise up and drown the earth; I will destroy cities and wipe out everyone who lives in them.
46.9: Forward, cavalry! Charge, chariots! Let the crack troops advance — Ethiopian and Libyan shield-bearers, Lydian archers bending their bows!
46.10: But that day belongs to the Lord Yahweh of hosts — the day he settles the score with his enemies. The sword will eat its fill and drink deep of their blood. The Lord Yahweh of hosts is staging a great slaughter by the Euphrates in the north.
46.11: Go to Gilead and get your medicines, O virgin daughter of Egypt! Stack remedy upon remedy — none of it will help. There is no cure for your wound.
46.12: Your humiliation has echoed among the nations; your cry has filled the earth. Warrior fell over warrior, and both went down together.
46.13: This is the word Yahweh spoke to Jeremiah the prophet concerning Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon's coming campaign against Egypt:
46.14: Broadcast it through Egypt! Shout it in Migdol, Memphis, and Tahpanhes: Stand to your post — be ready! The sword is already cutting its way around you!
46.15: Why have your mighty men been swept off their feet? They could not hold their ground — because Yahweh drove them down.
46.16: He sent them stumbling and collapsing over each other, and they cried out: Get up — let's go home! Back to our own people, our own land, away from that devouring sword!
46.17: There they gave him his true name: Pharaoh king of Egypt is nothing but bluster — he missed his moment.
46.18: As I live — the King declares, Yahweh of hosts is his name — as surely as Tabor rises above the hills and Carmel stands over the sea, he is coming.
46.19: Pack your bags for exile, all you who live in Egypt! Memphis will become a wasteland — a ghost city, empty of everyone.
46.20: Egypt is a beautiful heifer — but the stinging fly from the north has found her.
46.21: Even her mercenaries, penned and pampered like fattened calves — they too have turned tail and fled together. They would not stand their ground, for the day of their disaster arrived, the time of their accounting.
46.22: Egypt will hiss like a fleeing serpent as the army advances in full strength against her, axes raised like woodcutters come to level a forest.
46.23: They will cut down her forest — Yahweh declares it — even though it seems impossible to penetrate. For they come in numbers past counting, more than a swarm of locusts.
46.24: Egypt is shamed and handed over — delivered into the grip of a people from the north.
46.25: Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, says this: I am bringing judgment on Amon, the god of Thebes, on Pharaoh, on Egypt, on her gods and her kings — on Pharaoh and on every person who trusts in him.
46.26: I will hand them over to their enemies, into the power of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon and his commanders. But after that, Egypt will be inhabited again as it was in ancient times. Yahweh declares it.
46.27: But you — do not be afraid, Jacob my servant. Do not be terrified, Israel. I am going to save you from distant lands, your children from the land where they are captive. Jacob will come home to peace and safety, and no one will ever threaten him again.
46.28: Do not fear, Jacob my servant — Yahweh declares it — for I am with you. Every nation where I have scattered you I will destroy utterly. But you I will not destroy utterly. I will correct you, fairly and proportionately — but I will not let you go entirely unpunished.

40.1: This is the word Yahweh gave to Jeremiah after Nebuzaradan, the chief of the royal guard, released him from Ramah — where Jeremiah had been found bound in chains among all the exiles of Jerusalem and Judah being marched off to Babylon.
40.2: The chief of the guard took Jeremiah aside and said: Yahweh your God declared this catastrophe over this city.
40.3: Yahweh made it happen. He did exactly what he said he would do — because you sinned against him and refused to listen to his voice. That is why all this has fallen on you.
40.4: But here and now, I am removing the chains from your wrists. If you want to come with me to Babylon, come — I will take care of you well. But if you do not want to come with me, then do not. All the land lies open before you. Go wherever you see fit.
40.5: Or if you prefer to stay in the land, go to Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan, whom the king of Babylon has made governor over the towns of Judah, and settle there among the people — or go wherever you like.' The chief of the guard gave Jeremiah a food allowance and a parting gift and released him.
40.6: Jeremiah went to Gedaliah son of Ahikam at Mizpah and settled there with him, among all who were still left in the land.
40.7: When all the militia commanders scattered through the countryside — and their troops — heard that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedaliah son of Ahikam as governor over the land, and had placed under his care the men, women, children, and the very poorest of the people who had not been deported to Babylon,
40.8: they came to Gedaliah at Mizpah: Ishmael son of Nethaniah, Johanan and Jonathan sons of Kareah, Seraiah son of Tanhumeth, the sons of Ephai the Netophathite, Jezaniah son of the Maachathite — all of them with their men.
40.9: Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan made a solemn oath to them and to their men: Do not be afraid to submit to the Chaldeans. Settle in the land. Serve the king of Babylon. Do that, and it will be well with you.
40.10: I will stay here at Mizpah to deal with whatever Chaldean officials come to us. As for you — gather in the wine, the late harvest fruits, the oil. Store them up. Settle into the towns you now hold.
40.11: Meanwhile, all the Jews who had taken refuge in Moab, among the Ammonites, in Edom, and in every other land heard the news: the king of Babylon had left a remnant in Judah and set Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan over them.
40.12: They came back — all of them — from every place they had been scattered to. They came to the land of Judah, to Gedaliah at Mizpah. It was a season of abundance: they brought in great quantities of wine and late-harvest fruit.
40.13: Then Johanan son of Kareah and all the other militia commanders came to Gedaliah at Mizpah
40.14: and told him: Do you know that Baalis king of the Ammonites has dispatched Ishmael son of Nethaniah to kill you? But Gedaliah son of Ahikam would not believe it.
40.15: Then Johanan son of Kareah took Gedaliah aside at Mizpah and said quietly: Let me go and put Ishmael son of Nethaniah down. No one needs to know. Why should he kill you and undo everything — scatter all these people who have come back to you, and wipe out the last remnant of Judah?
40.16: But Gedaliah son of Ahikam said to Johanan son of Kareah: Do not do it. What you are saying about Ishmael is not true.

41.1: In the seventh month, Ishmael son of Nethaniah son of Elishama — a man of the royal line — came to Gedaliah son of Ahikam at Mizpah, bringing ten men with him. As they sat together eating at the table in Mizpah,
41.2: Ishmael son of Nethaniah and his ten men rose up and cut down Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan with the sword — murdering the man Babylon's king had made governor over the land.
41.3: Ishmael killed all the Jews who were with Gedaliah in Mizpah, and the Chaldean troops stationed there as well.
41.4: The next day — the day after the murder, before word had spread to anyone —
41.5: eighty men arrived from Shechem, Shiloh, and Samaria — beards shaved, clothes torn, bodies gashed — pilgrims bearing grain offerings and incense for Yahweh's temple.
41.6: Ishmael son of Nethaniah went out from Mizpah to meet them, weeping as he walked. When he reached them he said: Come and see Gedaliah son of Ahikam.
41.7: But once they were inside the town, Ishmael son of Nethaniah and his men butchered them and threw their bodies into a pit.
41.8: But ten of the men spoke up: Do not kill us. We have stores hidden in the field — wheat, barley, oil, honey. So Ishmael spared them and did not kill them with the others.
41.9: The pit where Ishmael threw the bodies was the great cistern that King Asa had built long ago as a fortification against Baasha king of Israel. Ishmael son of Nethaniah filled it with the dead.
41.10: Then Ishmael rounded up everyone left in Mizpah — including the royal daughters and all the people Nebuzaradan the guard commander had placed in Gedaliah's care. He took them captive and headed out toward Ammonite territory.
41.11: When Johanan son of Kareah and all the militia commanders with him heard what Ishmael son of Nethaniah had done — every atrocity of it —
41.12: they gathered all their men and set out to intercept Ishmael son of Nethaniah. They caught up with him at the great pool of Gibeon.
41.13: When all the captives Ishmael had taken saw Johanan son of Kareah and the commanders coming with their men, they were flooded with relief.
41.14: Every captive Ishmael had taken from Mizpah turned around and went to Johanan son of Kareah.
41.15: But Ishmael son of Nethaniah slipped away from Johanan with eight men and escaped to the Ammonites.
41.16: Johanan son of Kareah and all the commanders with him gathered up everyone they had rescued from Ishmael son of Nethaniah — soldiers, women, children, court officials — everyone Ishmael had taken from Mizpah after murdering Gedaliah son of Ahikam, and whom Johanan had now recovered at Gibeon.
41.17: They set out and camped at Geruth Chimham near Bethlehem, with Egypt as their destination —
41.18: afraid of the Chaldeans, because Ishmael son of Nethaniah had murdered Gedaliah son of Ahikam — the man Babylon's king had placed in charge of the land. They feared the Chaldean reprisal.

42.1: Then all the militia commanders — Johanan son of Kareah, Jezaniah son of Hoshaiah, every one of them — with all the people, from the smallest to the greatest, came forward
42.2: and said to Jeremiah the prophet: Please — hear our request. Pray to Yahweh your God for us, for this whole remnant. As you can see with your own eyes, we are barely anyone. Once we were many; now we are a handful.
42.3: Ask Yahweh your God to show us the road we should take and what we should do when we get there.
42.4: Jeremiah the prophet said to them: I have heard you. I will bring your request before Yahweh your God exactly as you have stated it. Whatever answer Yahweh gives, I will tell you every word of it — I will hold nothing back.
42.5: Then they said to Jeremiah: Let Yahweh be a true and reliable witness against us if we fail to do everything Yahweh your God sends you back to tell us.
42.6: Good news or bad news — whatever it is, we will obey the voice of Yahweh our God, to whom we are sending you. We do this so that things may go well with us — because we have obeyed Yahweh our God.
42.7: Ten days passed. Then Yahweh's word came to Jeremiah.
42.8: Jeremiah called Johanan son of Kareah, all the commanders who were with him, and all the people — from the smallest to the greatest —
42.9: and said to them: This is what Yahweh, the God of Israel, says — the one to whom you sent me to lay your request:
42.10: If you will stay in this land, I will build you up and not pull you down; I will plant you and not uproot you. For I am turning from the disaster I brought upon you.
42.11: Do not be afraid of the king of Babylon — I know you are afraid of him. Do not fear him. Yahweh declares it: I am with you to save you and to pull you free from his grip.
42.12: I will move him to show you mercy, so that you may come home to your own land.
42.13: But if you say: We will not stay in this land — refusing to obey Yahweh your God —
42.14: — saying instead: No, we are going to Egypt, where there is no war, where we will not hear the alarm of battle, where we will not go hungry — then
42.15: then hear the word of Yahweh, remnant of Judah. Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, says this: If you are set on going to Egypt, if you intend to settle there as immigrants,
42.16: then the very sword you are fleeing will catch up to you in Egypt. The very famine you dread will follow you right into Egypt. And there you will die.
42.17: Every person who turns their face toward Egypt and goes there to live will die — by sword, by famine, by plague. Not one will survive or escape the disaster I bring upon them.
42.18: For this is what Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, says: Just as I poured out my anger and fury on the people of Jerusalem, I will pour my fury out on you when you enter Egypt. You will become a byword of contempt — a thing people shudder at, a curse, a disgrace. And you will never see this land again.
42.19: Yahweh has spoken to you, remnant of Judah: Do not go to Egypt. Take this to heart today: I have given you a clear warning.
42.20: You have been acting in bad faith from the start. You sent me to Yahweh your God with those words: Pray for us. Tell us what Yahweh our God says. We will do it.
42.21: Today I delivered his answer — every word of it. And you will not obey the voice of Yahweh your God. Not in a single thing he sent me to tell you.
42.22: So know this with certainty: You will die by sword, by famine, by plague — in the very place you are determined to go.

47.1: This is Yahweh's word that came to Jeremiah the prophet against the Philistines — before Pharaoh struck Gaza.
47.2: This is what Yahweh says: Look — waters are surging up from the north, swelling into a flood that will overwhelm the land and everything in it, every city and all its people. Men will cry out; every person in the land will wail.
47.3: At the thunder of stampeding hoofs, the charge of chariots, the rattling of their wheels — fathers will not even look back for their children. Their hands hang limp in helpless dread.
47.4: For the day has come for Yahweh to ruin all the Philistines and to cut off every last ally — from Tyre and Sidon there will be no help. Yahweh is destroying the Philistines, every last survivor from the shores of Caphtor.
47.5: Gaza is shaved bare; Ashkelon lies silent and broken. O you few left in the valley — how long will you go on slashing yourselves in grief?
47.6: O sword of Yahweh — how long? When will you finally rest? Go back into your sheath. Be still. Stop.
47.7: But how can it rest when Yahweh himself has given it its assignment? Against Ashkelon, against the seacoast — there he has sent it to its work.

48.1: Concerning Moab, this is what Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, says: Woe to Nebo — it is laid waste! Kiriathaim is shamed and taken. The fortress is shamed and broken.
48.2: Moab's fame is finished. In Heshbon they are plotting its end: Come — let us erase it from the roll of nations. And you, Madmen, you too will be silenced. The sword is coming after you.
48.3: From Horonaim comes a shriek — ruin and catastrophic destruction!
48.4: Moab is shattered. Even her youngest cry aloud.
48.5: Going up to Luhith, there is nothing but weeping all the way. Going down to Horonaim, the enemy hears the shriek of ruin.
48.6: Run! Save your lives! Be like a bare desert shrub — alone, stripped down, surviving on nothing.
48.7: Because you put your confidence in your achievements and your accumulated wealth — you too will be seized. Chemosh will march into exile with his priests and his officials, all of them together.
48.8: The destroyer will sweep through every city. Not one will escape. The valley will be laid waste, the plain destroyed — exactly as Yahweh has spoken.
48.9: Give Moab wings so she can fly — for her cities will be emptied out, no one left to dwell in them.
48.10: Cursed is the man who carries out Yahweh's work halfheartedly. Cursed is the man who pulls back his sword from blood.
48.11: Moab has been at ease since his youth — like wine left untouched, settled on its sediment. He has never been poured from vessel to vessel, never sent into exile. His taste is unchanged; his scent has never been stirred. He has never known what it is to be tested.
48.12: So look — days are coming, Yahweh declares, when I will send men who will tip him over. They will pour him out, drain every vessel, and smash every jar.
48.13: Moab will be shamed by Chemosh — just as the house of Israel was shamed by Bethel, the god they had counted on to save them.
48.14: How can you say, 'We are warriors — men proven in battle'?
48.15: Moab is ruined. His cities have been stormed; his finest young men have gone down to slaughter. The King speaks — and his name is Yahweh of hosts.
48.16: Moab's doom is near — his ruin is racing to meet him.
48.17: All who surround him — mourn for him! All who know his name — say it: How the mighty scepter is broken! How the glorious staff lies shattered!
48.18: Come down from your throne of pride and sit in thirst, daughter Dibon — the destroyer of Moab has come against you and laid your strongholds in ruins.
48.19: Take your post on the road and watch, you who live in Aroer. Stop the one who flees, stop the refugee, and ask: What happened?
48.20: Moab is shamed — broken and humiliated. Wail! Cry out! Spread the word at the Arnon: Moab is devastated.
48.21: Judgment has come to the plateau — to Holon, to Jahzah, to Mephaath,
48.22: to Dibon, to Nebo, to Beth-diblathaim,
48.23: to Kiriathaim, to Beth-gamul, to Beth-meon,
48.24: to Kerioth, to Bozrah — to every city of Moab, near and far.
48.25: Moab's horn is hacked off. His arm is broken. Yahweh declares it.
48.26: Get him drunk — for he lifted himself up against Yahweh. Moab will reel and wallow in his own vomit, and he will become a laughingstock.
48.27: Did you not mock Israel? Was he some thief caught in the act, that you wagged your head at him every time his name came up?
48.28: Abandon your cities and take refuge in the rocky crags, O people of Moab. Be like the dove that builds its nest in the face of the gorge, deep in the cleft of the rock.
48.29: We have heard of Moab's pride — and it is immense: his arrogance, his insolence, his self-exaltation, his haughtiness, the towering conceit of his heart. Five words — none of them adequate.
48.30: I know his rage, declares Yahweh — but it is hollow. All his boasting amounts to nothing. It will not stand.
48.31: Therefore I will wail for Moab. I will cry out for all of Moab. I will mourn for the men of Kir-hareseth.
48.32: O vine of Sibmah — I weep for you more than Jazer's own tears. Your branches once spread across the sea, reaching to the sea of Jazer. Now the destroyer has come crashing down on your summer harvest and your vintage.
48.33: Joy and celebration have been stripped from the fertile fields of Moab. I have silenced the wine in the presses. No one treads the grapes with harvest shouts now — the only sounds are not of joy.
48.34: From Heshbon's cry the sound reaches Elealeh and Jahaz; from Zoar to Horonaim it rings out like the bellow of a young heifer. And even the waters of Nimrim will run dry.
48.35: I will put an end in Moab — Yahweh declares it — to every man who climbs the high places to sacrifice, to every man who burns incense to his gods.
48.36: That is why my heart wails for Moab like a mourning flute — wails for the men of Kir-hareseth — for the wealth they built up is all gone.
48.37: Every head is shaved bare, every beard is shorn. Gashes on every hand. Sackcloth on every waist.
48.38: On every rooftop in Moab, in every street — nothing but mourning. I have smashed Moab like a vessel that no one wants. Yahweh declares it.
48.39: How it lies in pieces! How Moab has turned and fled in shame! So Moab becomes a mockery and a horror to every nation around him.
48.40: For this is what Yahweh says: Watch — he swoops like an eagle and spreads his wings over Moab.
48.41: Kerioth is captured; the fortresses are seized. On that day Moab's warriors will have hearts like a woman in the grip of labor.
48.42: Moab will be wiped out as a people — because he exalted himself against Yahweh.
48.43: Dread and pit and snare are set against you, O people of Moab. Yahweh declares it.
48.44: Run from the dread and you fall into the pit. Climb out of the pit and the snare catches you. For I will bring upon Moab the year of their reckoning. Yahweh declares it.
48.45: The refugees stopped, spent, in the shadow of Heshbon — but fire burst from Heshbon, a flame from within Sihon, devouring the frontier of Moab, the heads of the proud and noisy sons. The ancient taunt finds its fulfillment.
48.46: Woe to you, O Moab! The people of Chemosh are ruined — your sons hauled off as captives, your daughters marched away into exile.
48.47: Yet in the days to come I will reverse the fate of Moab. Yahweh declares it. This far — and no further — is the judgment of Moab.

49.1: Concerning the Ammonites — Yahweh says this: Has Israel no sons? No heir to the land? Why then has Milcom seized Gad's inheritance and his people occupied its towns?
49.2: The days are coming — Yahweh declares it — when the war cry will ring out against Rabbah in Ammon. That city will be turned into a ruined heap and its surrounding towns burned to the ground. Then Israel will take back what was taken from her.
49.3: Wail, Heshbon — Ai is destroyed! Daughters of Rabbah, break out in mourning! Wrap yourselves in sackcloth; wail and run back and forth along the hedgerows. For Milcom himself is going into exile, priests and princes swept away with him.
49.4: Why are you so proud of your abundant valleys, O daughter who keeps turning away? You trusted in your riches and thought: Who could ever reach me?
49.5: I am bringing a terror down on you — the Lord Yahweh of hosts declares it — from every direction, from all your neighbors. You will be driven out, every person fleeing headlong, with no one to round up the refugees.
49.6: But after this, I will restore Ammon's fortunes. Yahweh declares it.
49.7: Concerning Edom — Yahweh of hosts says this: Is there no wisdom left in Teman? Has sound counsel disappeared from those who were once wise? Has their understanding simply evaporated?
49.8: Flee! Turn back! Take cover in the wilderness depths, you people of Dedan! For I am bringing Esau's disaster on him — the moment of his reckoning has arrived.
49.9: When grape-pickers work through a vineyard, they always leave something behind. When thieves break in at night, they take only what they need. But Edom will not be left even that much.
49.10: But I have stripped Esau completely bare. I have exposed every hiding place — there is nowhere left for him to conceal himself. His children are gone, his brothers gone, his neighbors gone. There is nothing left.
49.11: Leave your orphaned children — I will care for them. Your widows can put their trust in me.
49.12: Yahweh says this: Those who were not even supposed to drink the cup of judgment have had to drink it. Do you think you will escape without drinking? You will not. You will drain it.
49.13: I have sworn by my own name — Yahweh declares it — that Bozrah will become a desolation, a reproach, a ruin, and a curse. Every one of its towns will lie in permanent ruin.
49.14: I have heard a dispatch from Yahweh. A messenger is going out among the nations: Muster your forces and march against her! Prepare for battle!
49.15: I will cut you down to the smallest of nations — despised among all peoples.
49.16: Your reputation for terror has seduced you — that arrogance at the core of you. You live in the crannies of the cliffs; you hold the high ground. You have built your nest higher than the eagle — but I will drag you down from there. Yahweh declares it.
49.17: Edom will be left a horror. Everyone who passes through will recoil and hiss in disbelief at everything that has happened to it.
49.18: Like the catastrophic end of Sodom and Gomorrah and the towns around them — Yahweh says it — no one will ever settle there again. Not a single person will live there.
49.19: A lion rises from the tangled undergrowth of the Jordan valley and surges toward the rich pastureland — and just as suddenly I will drive Edom off. I will appoint whoever I choose to rule over her. For who is my equal? Who could summon me to account? What shepherd could face me and survive?
49.20: Hear, then, the strategy Yahweh has formed against Edom — the purposes he has shaped against the people of Teman: Even the weakest lamb will be dragged off, and the very pastures will be turned into wasteland around them.
49.21: The earth will tremble at the noise of Edom's collapse. Their cry will be heard as far away as the Red Sea.
49.22: He rises and swoops like an eagle, spreading his wings over Bozrah. On that day the hearts of Edom's bravest warriors will be like the heart of a woman writhing in the anguish of childbirth.
49.23: Concerning Damascus: Hamath and Arpad are thrown into confusion by the terrible news; they are sick with dread. Anxiety churns through them like a sea that will not quiet itself.
49.24: Damascus has gone limp. She turned to flee and was seized by panic. Anguish and birth-pangs have gripped her; she cannot stand.
49.25: How can the city that was once celebrated — the city that was my delight — end up like this?
49.26: That is why her young men are falling in the streets, and all her soldiers will be cut down on that day. Yahweh of hosts declares it.
49.27: I will set Damascus's walls ablaze, and the fire will devour every palace of Ben-hadad.
49.28: Concerning Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor that Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon crushed — Yahweh says this: Rise up! March against Kedar! Strip the eastern peoples bare!
49.29: Their tents and their flocks will be seized; their tent curtains, all their equipment, and their camels will be carried off — and someone will shout over them: Terror on every side!
49.30: Flee! Get out! Take cover in the wilderness, you people of Hazor — Yahweh declares it — for Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon has made his plans against you and set his strategy in motion.
49.31: March against a nation that lives in ease — smug and secure, Yahweh declares — with no city gates, no bars, living out there alone in open country.
49.32: Their camels will become plunder and their great herds a prize of war. I will scatter those who trim their hair at the temples into every wind — and their disaster will come from every direction. Yahweh declares it.
49.33: Hazor will become a den of jackals, a permanent wasteland. No one will ever live there again — no one, ever.
49.34: This is the word Yahweh gave to Jeremiah the prophet concerning Elam, at the start of Zedekiah king of Judah's reign:
49.35: Yahweh of hosts says this: I am going to break the bow of Elam — the weapon that is the very heart of their power.
49.36: I will summon the four winds from the four corners of heaven against Elam and scatter its people in every direction. There will not be a single nation on earth that does not receive Elam's refugees.
49.37: I will break Elam apart before their enemies — before every faction that wants them destroyed. I am bringing disaster on them, the full force of my fury. Yahweh declares it. I will send the sword after them until I have finished what I started.
49.38: I will set up my throne in Elam and sweep away their king and all his officials from there. Yahweh declares it.
49.39: But in the days to come I will restore Elam's fortunes. Yahweh declares it.

50.1: This is the word Yahweh spoke concerning Babylon and the land of the Chaldeans, through Jeremiah the prophet:
50.2: Broadcast it to the nations — proclaim it! Raise the signal banner and shout it out — hold nothing back: Babylon has fallen! Bel is shamed! Merodach is overthrown! Every idol disgraced, every image smashed!
50.3: A nation is coming up from the north against her — it will leave her land a desolation. No one will live there. Man and beast alike will flee; all will be gone.
50.4: In those days and at that time — Yahweh declares it — the people of Israel and the people of Judah will come together, weeping as they go, seeking Yahweh their God.
50.5: They will ask directions to Zion with their faces set toward home: Come — let us bind ourselves to Yahweh in an unbreakable covenant that will never be forgotten.
50.6: My people have been lost sheep. Their shepherds led them off the path — sent them wandering across the mountains, from peak to hill, with no memory of where home was.
50.7: Everyone who found them devoured them. Their enemies said: 'We are not guilty — they sinned against Yahweh, the true home and resting place, Yahweh, the hope of their ancestors.'
50.8: Get out of Babylon! Leave the land of the Chaldeans! Be the first ones out — like the lead goats heading up the flock.
50.9: For I am rousing an assembly of great nations from the north and marshaling them against Babylon. They will take their positions; she will be captured from that direction. Their arrows are those of a master warrior — not one will fly back unspent.
50.10: Chaldea will be thoroughly looted. Everyone who plunders her will have their fill. Yahweh declares it.
50.11: You plundered my inheritance and celebrated. You frolicked like a well-fed calf on fresh grass and roared like bulls in their prime —
50.12: — your mother is ashamed. The nation that bore you will be humiliated. She will end up the most despised among all nations — a wilderness, a parched land, a desert.
50.13: Babylon will be uninhabited — Yahweh's fury will make sure of that — completely desolate. Everyone who passes through will recoil and hiss at everything that has struck her.
50.14: Take your positions around Babylon — every archer in place — and shoot! Don't hold back a single arrow, for she has sinned against Yahweh.
50.15: Raise the battle shout against her on every side! She has surrendered. Her defenses have crumbled; her walls have been torn down. This is Yahweh's vengeance — take it. Give her exactly what she gave everyone else.
50.16: Cut off every sower from Babylon, every harvester who swings the sickle at reaping time — before the oppressor's sword, every foreigner will break and run for home.
50.17: Israel is a scattered flock — lions have been driving it apart. First it was the king of Assyria who devoured them; and last of all came Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon and crushed their bones.
50.18: Therefore Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, says this: I am going to punish the king of Babylon and his land, just as I punished the king of Assyria.
50.19: And I will bring Israel home — back to the pasture that belongs to him. He will graze on Carmel and Bashan; he will eat his fill on the hills of Ephraim and in Gilead.
50.20: In those days and at that time — Yahweh declares it — someone will search for Israel's iniquity and find nothing. Someone will look for Judah's sins and they will not exist. For I will forgive everyone I have preserved.
50.21: March against the land of Merathaim and against the people of Pekod. Cut them down and devote every last thing to destruction — Yahweh declares it — carry out every order I have given.
50.22: The battle is raging across the land — destruction on a massive scale!
50.23: The hammer of the whole earth — shattered and broken to pieces! How has Babylon become a horror among the nations?
50.24: I set a trap for you and you fell in, Babylon — you never even knew it was there. You were caught and seized. That is what you get for going to war against Yahweh.
50.25: Yahweh has thrown open his armory and brought out the weapons of his fury — because the Lord Yahweh of hosts has a task to complete in the land of the Chaldeans.
50.26: Come against her from the outermost edge! Break open her storehouses! Heap her up and wipe out every last bit — leave absolutely nothing!
50.27: Cut down every last one of her bulls — drive them down to slaughter! Woe to them — their day has arrived, the hour of their final accounting!
50.28: Listen — the sound of the fugitives, the people escaping from Babylon, making their way to Zion to announce Yahweh our God's vengeance: his vengeance for his temple.
50.29: Summon every archer against Babylon — every bowstring drawn! Surround her completely; no one escapes. Pay her back in full for everything she has done. She has been arrogant against Yahweh himself — against the Holy One of Israel.
50.30: That is why her young men will fall in the streets and all her soldiers will be silenced on that day. Yahweh declares it.
50.31: I am against you — you who are the most arrogant of all — the Lord Yahweh of hosts declares it. Your day has arrived; the moment of your reckoning has come.
50.32: The most arrogant one will stumble and fall — and no one will be there to help him up. I will set his cities ablaze, and the fire will devour everything around him.
50.33: Yahweh of hosts says this: The people of Israel are oppressed, and so are the people of Judah — held tight in the grip of every one of their captors, who refuse to let them go.
50.34: But their Redeemer is powerful — Yahweh of hosts is his name. He will argue their case with full force. He will bring rest to the land — but turmoil without end to the people of Babylon.
50.35: The sword is drawn against the Chaldeans — Yahweh declares it — against the people of Babylon, against her officials and her wise counselors!
50.36: The sword comes for her court diviners — they will rave like madmen! The sword for her warriors — they will collapse in terror!
50.37: The sword against her horses, against her chariots, against every mercenary in her ranks — they will lose every drop of their fighting spirit! The sword against her treasuries — they will be looted!
50.38: Drought on her waters — let them be drained dry! For this is a land of idols, and her idols have driven its people out of their minds.
50.39: Wild desert animals will nest in Babylon alongside jackals; ostriches will make their home there. It will never be inhabited again — never, for generation after generation.
50.40: Just as God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and the towns around them — Yahweh declares it — no one will ever live in Babylon again. Not ever.
50.41: Look — a people is coming from the north: a great nation and many kings, roused from the farthest corners of the earth.
50.42: They grip their bows and their spears — cruel soldiers without a trace of mercy. Their voices thunder like the sea. They come mounted on horses, every one in battle formation — against you, daughter of Babylon.
50.43: When the king of Babylon heard the reports about them, his hands fell limp. Anguish gripped him — writhing like a woman in the throes of labor.
50.44: A lion rises from the tangled undergrowth of the Jordan valley and surges toward rich pastureland — and just as suddenly I will drive Babylon's rulers off. I will appoint whoever I choose to rule over her. For who is my equal? Who could call me to account? What shepherd could face me and live?
50.45: So hear the strategy Yahweh has formed against Babylon — the purposes he has shaped against the land of the Chaldeans: Even the weakest lamb will be dragged off, and their very pastures will be turned into wasteland around them.
50.46: At the noise of Babylon's fall, the earth itself will shake. Her cry will echo among all the nations.

51.1: This is what Yahweh says: I am sending a destroying wind against Babylon — against those who dwell in Chaldea.
51.2: I will send threshers against Babylon — men who will winnow her clean and leave her land empty. They will come at her from every direction when disaster falls.
51.3: Let the archer draw against the one who draws. Let the warrior rise against the armored fighter. Spare none of Babylon's young men — consecrate her whole force to destruction.
51.4: The dead will lie across Chaldea's land, the sword-pierced in her streets.
51.5: Israel and Judah have not been abandoned by their God, Yahweh of hosts — even though their land is saturated with guilt before Israel's Holy God.
51.6: Get out of Babylon! Flee now — save your life! Do not be swept away in the punishment she deserves. This is Yahweh's hour of vengeance; he is paying her back in full.
51.7: Babylon was a cup of gold in Yahweh's hand — it made the whole earth drunk. The nations drank from it and went out of their minds.
51.8: Babylon has suddenly fallen — shattered. Wail for her! Bring medicine for her wound. Perhaps she can be healed.
51.9: We tried to heal Babylon — she cannot be healed. Abandon her. Let every man go back to his own country. Her sentence has piled up to heaven itself, stacked to the very skies.
51.10: Yahweh has made our case good — he has vindicated us. Come, let us proclaim in Zion what Yahweh our God has done.
51.11: Sharpen the arrows! Fill the quivers! Yahweh has stirred the resolve of the Median kings — his plan is to ruin Babylon completely. This is Yahweh's vengeance — payback for what was done to his temple.
51.12: Plant the battle standard at Babylon's walls. Strengthen the watch. Post the guards. Set the ambushes. Yahweh has done exactly what he planned — everything he said about Babylon's people he is now carrying out.
51.13: You who sit on many waters, fat with treasure — your end has come. The cord of your life has been cut through.
51.14: Yahweh of hosts has sworn by his own life: I will pour men over you like a swarm of locusts, and they will shout their war cry against you.
51.15: He is the one who made the earth by his power, set the world in place by his wisdom, and stretched out the heavens by his understanding.
51.16: When he speaks, the heavens churn with water. He lifts clouds from the far edges of the earth, sends lightning with the rain, and lets the wind loose from his own storehouses.
51.17: Every human being is too dull to grasp this. Every idol-maker is put to shame by what he made — his poured image is a lie, a thing with no breath in it at all.
51.18: They are nothing — products of illusion. When the time of their reckoning comes, they will be swept away.
51.19: The God of Jacob is nothing like these idols. He made everything that exists. Israel is his own inheritance, his chosen people — Yahweh of hosts is his name.
51.20: You are my hammer of war, my weapon of destruction — with you I shatter nations, with you I break kingdoms apart;
51.21: with you I shatter the horse and rider, with you I break apart the chariot and the one who drives it;
51.22: with you I shatter man and woman, old man and boy, young man and girl;
51.23: with you I shatter the shepherd and his flock, the farmer and his oxen, governors and commanders alike.
51.24: I will pay Babylon and all of Chaldea back in full for what they did to Zion — and you will watch it happen, declares Yahweh.
51.25: I am against you, destroying mountain — you who have ruined the whole earth, declares Yahweh. I will stretch out my hand against you, roll you down from your rocky height, and make you a blackened, burned-out mountain.
51.26: Not one stone of you will ever be taken for a cornerstone or a foundation. You will be a ruin for ever, declares Yahweh.
51.27: Raise the battle standard across the land! Sound the trumpet among the nations! Mobilize the nations against her. Call up Ararat, Minni, and Ashkenaz against her. Appoint a commander. Bring horses thick as bristling locusts.
51.28: Mobilize the nations against her — the Median kings with their governors and officials, every territory they rule.
51.29: The land shudders and twists in pain, for Yahweh's plans against Babylon are fixed: to make it a wasteland where no one lives.
51.30: Babylon's warriors have stopped fighting. They crouch in their fortresses, their strength gone — they have become like women. Her buildings are burning. Her gate-bars are broken.
51.31: Messenger after messenger runs to report to the king of Babylon — runner meets runner, courier meets courier — bringing the word: your city has fallen on every side;
51.32: the river crossings are cut off, the marshes are burning, and the soldiers are in full panic.
51.33: For this is what Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, says: Daughter Babylon is like a threshing floor at harvest time — the grain is being trodden. Just a little while more, and the time for her own harvest will come.
51.34: Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon devoured me and crushed me. He made me into an empty jar. He swallowed me like a sea-dragon, gorged himself on my best things, and spat me out empty.
51.35: "Let the violence done to me and mine fall on Babylon," Zion's people will say. "Let my blood be on Chaldea's head," Jerusalem will say.
51.36: Therefore this is what Yahweh says: I will argue your case. I will take vengeance for you. I will drain Babylon's sea dry and stop her springs from flowing.
51.37: Babylon will become a rubble-heap — a den for jackals, a place so ruined it will make people shudder and hiss, empty of every living soul.
51.38: They will roar together like lions, snarling like lion cubs.
51.39: When they are at full heat, I will set a banquet before them and make them drunk — let them celebrate for a moment — and then they will fall into a sleep from which they will never rise, declares Yahweh.
51.40: I will lead them to slaughter like lambs — like rams and goats going to the knife.
51.41: Sheshach — Babylon itself — is taken! The city the whole earth praised is seized! How has Babylon become a thing of horror among the nations!
51.42: The sea has risen over Babylon — she is buried under its crashing, roaring waves.
51.43: Her cities are horror and ruin — a cracked, empty desert where no one lives and no traveler passes through.
51.44: I will punish Bel in Babylon and make him vomit out what he has swallowed — all the plunder gorged from the nations. They will no longer stream to him in tribute. The wall of Babylon has fallen.
51.45: Come out of her, my people! Save your lives from Yahweh's burning anger!
51.46: Do not lose heart. Do not be terrified at the rumors sweeping the land — one report coming this year, another the next, with violence everywhere and rulers fighting rulers —
51.47: know this: the days are coming when I will call Babylon's idols to account. Her whole land will be shamed, and her dead will lie where they fell — inside her.
51.48: Then heaven and earth and everything in them will break into shouts of joy over Babylon — for her destroyers will sweep down from the north, declares Yahweh.
51.49: Babylon must fall for the sake of Israel's dead — just as all the earth's dead fell because of Babylon.
51.50: You who survived the sword — go, don't stand there! Remember Yahweh from far away. Let Jerusalem fill your minds:
51.51: "We are shamed — we have heard the mockery. Dishonor has covered our faces. Foreigners have walked into the holy places of Yahweh's house."
51.52: But those days are coming, declares Yahweh — I will call her idols to account, and from one end of Babylon to the other the dying will groan.
51.53: Even if Babylon climbed up to heaven itself — even if she fortified the very heights — destroyers would still come from me against her, declares Yahweh.
51.54: Hear it — a shriek from Babylon! The crash of catastrophic destruction from Chaldea!
51.55: Yahweh is ravaging Babylon and silencing her roar. The attacking waves crash like a flood, and the shout of the assault rises to the sky.
51.56: The destroyer has come against Babylon. Her soldiers are captured. Their bows are snapped. For Yahweh is a God of payback — he always repays.
51.57: I will make drunk every one of Babylon's officials and sages, her governors and commanders and soldiers. They will fall into a sleep they will never wake from — declares the King whose name is Yahweh of hosts.
51.58: This is what Yahweh of hosts says: Babylon's massive walls will be leveled and her great gates burned to the ground. All the labor of the peoples will come to nothing — the nations have worked themselves to exhaustion for fuel.
51.59: This is the commission Jeremiah the prophet gave to Seraiah son of Neriah son of Mahseiah, when he traveled with King Zedekiah of Judah to Babylon in the fourth year of his reign. Seraiah was the head quartermaster.
51.60: Jeremiah wrote out in a single scroll everything that would come upon Babylon — every word recorded here.
51.61: Jeremiah instructed Seraiah: When you reach Babylon, read every one of these words aloud.
51.62: Then say: O Yahweh, you yourself declared this place finished — cut off so completely that neither person nor animal will ever live here again. It will be desolate forever.
51.63: When you finish reading the scroll, tie a stone to it and fling it into the middle of the Euphrates.
51.64: Then say: This is how Babylon will sink — never to rise again — because of the disaster I am bringing upon it. They will sink exhausted. Here ends the word of Jeremiah.

52.1: Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he came to the throne and reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. His mother was Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.
52.2: He did what was evil in Yahweh's eyes, following Jehoiakim's example exactly.
52.3: All of this — Jerusalem, Judah — came about through the anger of Yahweh, until at last he drove them out of his presence. Then Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
52.4: In the ninth year of his reign, on the tenth day of the tenth month, Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon came with his whole army against Jerusalem and laid siege to it, throwing up earthworks against it on every side.
52.5: The city remained besieged all the way to the eleventh year of Zedekiah's reign.
52.6: By the ninth day of the fourth month the famine gripping the city was catastrophic — there was nothing left to eat for any of the people.
52.7: Then the wall was breached. Every soldier fled — slipping out of the city in the dark through the gate between the two walls beside the king's garden, even with the Chaldeans surrounding the city on every side. They headed for the Jordan Valley.
52.8: The Chaldean army gave chase and caught Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho. His entire army had scattered and left him.
52.9: They seized Zedekiah and brought him to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath, where judgment was pronounced on him.
52.10: The king of Babylon had Zedekiah's sons killed before his eyes, and all the officials of Judah were executed at Riblah as well.
52.11: Then Nebuchadrezzar put out Zedekiah's eyes, bound him in bronze chains, brought him to Babylon, and kept him in prison until he died.
52.12: On the tenth day of the fifth month, in the nineteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, Nebuzaradan, captain of the guard and personal servant of the king, arrived in Jerusalem.
52.13: He burned the house of Yahweh, the royal palace, and every house in Jerusalem — every significant building reduced to ash.
52.14: The whole Chaldean force serving under the captain of the guard demolished every wall surrounding Jerusalem.
52.15: Nebuzaradan deported some of the poorest remaining in the city, those who had deserted to Babylon, and whatever skilled craftsmen were left.
52.16: Nebuzaradan left behind only the poorest of the poor — people to tend the vineyards and work the fields.
52.17: The Chaldeans smashed the bronze pillars, the bronze stands, and the great bronze sea that stood in Yahweh's house, and hauled all the bronze to Babylon.
52.18: They stripped out every bronze utensil of the temple service: the pots, shovels, snuffers, sprinkling bowls, incense dishes — everything.
52.19: The captain of the guard took everything else — the small bowls, fire pans, basins, pots, lampstands, incense dishes, and libation bowls. Gold items he took as gold; silver items as silver.
52.20: The two great pillars, the bronze sea with its twelve bronze bulls beneath it, and the movable stands that Solomon had made for Yahweh's house — all this bronze was beyond measuring.
52.21: Each pillar stood eighteen cubits high with a circumference of twelve cubits — four fingers thick, and hollow inside.
52.22: Each pillar was crowned with a bronze capital five cubits high, encircled all around with bronze latticework and pomegranates. The second pillar was identical.
52.23: Ninety-six pomegranates could be seen from the sides; the full count on the surrounding lattice was one hundred.
52.24: The captain of the guard arrested Seraiah the chief priest, Zephaniah the deputy priest, and the three men who kept the threshold.
52.25: From the city he seized an officer who had commanded the troops, seven of the king's advisers who were still in the city, the secretary in charge of military conscription, and sixty ordinary citizens found there.
52.26: Nebuzaradan brought all of them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
52.27: There at Riblah in Hamath the king of Babylon had every one of them executed. So Judah was driven from its land into exile.
52.28: Here is the register of those Nebuchadrezzar carried into exile: in his seventh year, 3,023 Judeans.
52.29: In his eighteenth year, 832 persons from Jerusalem.
52.30: In his twenty-third year, Nebuzaradan carried away 745 more Judeans. The total for all three deportations: 4,600 persons.
52.31: In the thirty-seventh year of Jehoiachin's exile — on the twenty-fifth day of the twelfth month — Evil-merodach king of Babylon, in the very year he took the throne, showed mercy to Jehoiachin king of Judah and brought him out of prison.
52.32: He spoke kindly to him and gave him a place of honor higher than all the other captive kings in Babylon.
52.33: Jehoiachin exchanged his prison clothes for royal clothing and ate at the king's table every day for the rest of his life.
52.34: A daily provision was assigned to him by the king of Babylon — a fixed portion each day — for every remaining day of his life until he died. The Davidic line was not extinguished.
